name: Santoña Marshes and Collado de Asón Loop
name_original: Argoños, Collado de Asón, Ramales de la Victoria, Coterillo, Marismas de Santoña – ruta circular
description: This difficult 63.6-mile road cycling route offers challenging climbs, diverse scenery, and 6313 feet of elevation gain in Cantabria.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Argoños are set within the diverse landscapes of Cantabria, Spain, characterized by a blend of coastal areas, extensive marshes, and varied inland terrain. The region features a dynamic environment where mountains meet the sea, offering both flat coastal paths and more challenging ascents. This area is an integral part of the Natural Park of the Marshes of Santoña, Victoria, and Joyel, providing unique natural features for cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Argoños The most popular road cycling route is Santoña Marshes and Collado de Asón Loop, a difficult 63.6 miles (102.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation changes and diverse scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Marismas de Santoña, Escalante, San Mamés de Meruela – circular from Santoña, an easy 15.3 miles (24.6 km) path. This route offers views of the Santoña Marshes and coastal areas. Local road cyclists also love the Nice small road – Playa de la Salvé loop from Noja, a 30.0 miles (48.3 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Argoños is defined by coastal paths, extensive marshes, and varied terrain where mountains meet the sea. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Argoños are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 5,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Argoños's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Argoños?
A: There are over 100 road cycling routes around Argoños, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging options to explore the diverse landscapes of Cantabria.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Argoños?
A: The region offers a dynamic environment where mountains meet the sea. You can expect a blend of flat coastal paths, extensive marshlands, and more challenging ascents into varied inland terrain. Routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005118280/santona-marshes-and-collado-de-ason-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santoña Marshes and Collado de Asón Loop&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation changes, while others provide gentler rides.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly road cycling routes in the Argoños area?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families or those seeking a more relaxed ride. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012734595/marismas-de-santona-escalante-san-mames-de-meruela-circular-from-santona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marismas de Santoña, Escalante, San Mamés de Meruela – circular from Santoña&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 15.3-mile (24.6 km) path offering views of the Santoña Marshes and coastal areas. Cantabria also features "Vías Verdes" (Greenways), former railway lines converted into accessible, flat cycling routes ideal for all ages.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Argoños is part of the Natural Park of the Marshes of Santoña, Victoria, and Joyel, offering unique ecological sights. You can also cycle along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4977738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laredo Promenade&lt;/a&gt; for expansive ocean views. Other notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2984253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro del Pescador&lt;/a&gt;, offering extensive coastal views, and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/534543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent to Faro del Caballo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Argoños?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40370992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santoña Marshes – Marshes Viewpoint loop from Santoña&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 43.8 km (27.2 miles) route that allows you to return to your starting point after enjoying the scenery.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Argoños?
A: The road cycling routes around Argoños are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse scenery, from coastal paths to marshlands and inland climbs, and the variety of routes available for different abilities.

Q: Is it possible to find challenging road cycling routes near Argoños?
A: Absolutely. For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005118280/santona-marshes-and-collado-de-ason-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santoña Marshes and Collado de Asón Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes and cover over 100 km, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views, especially those along the coast or through the Natural Park of the Marshes of Santoña, Victoria, and Joyel. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2984253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro del Pescador&lt;/a&gt; provides extensive coastal vistas, and routes around Isla often feature views of the estuary, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32102620" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful curvy road up to Isla – View of the Estuary loop from Noja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Argoños?
A: The Cantabrian coast generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures and less crowded routes. While specific weather can vary, the region's diverse landscapes are enjoyable across different seasons.

Q: Are there any unique natural parks or areas to explore by bike beyond the immediate Argoños vicinity?
A: Yes, beyond the Natural Park of the Marshes of Santoña, Victoria, and Joyel, Cantabria boasts several other stunning natural parks. These include Oyambre Natural Park with its coastal landscapes, Saja-Besaya Natural Park known for its forests, and Liencres Dunes Natural Park. A short distance away, &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cabarceno_Natural_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabárceno Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; also offers over 20 km of roads suitable for cycling, allowing you to see animals in a semi-wild environment.

Q: Where can I find parking for cyclists in Argoños?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists are not detailed, Argoños, as a municipality, generally offers public parking options. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient and secure places to park your vehicle before starting your ride.
tips: The Collado de Asón is an 11km section with a drop of 530m and an average gradient of 4.81%. The asphalt is perfect, with wooden fences on the curves, horizontal marking on the margins, etc. The traffic is light.
Next to the Asón River, Coterillo is a town in the municipality of Ampuero, 3 kilometers from the municipal capital, Ampuero. It has few inhabitants.
An easy site, but demanding in some points, for the road bike. With impressive views.

This natural park is the most important wetland on the Cantabrian coast. Its 6,679 hectares are full of unique marine fauna and are home to more than 121 species of waterfowl. The tides constantly change this landscape that can be seen from the different shores and from the bridge that crosses it.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a 'Hard road ride' and demands very good fitness from cyclists. It features a significant elevation gain of over 1,900 meters across approximately 102 kilometers.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular racebike route?
faqs.answer: On average, this route takes around 5 hours and 34 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on your fitness level and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain. While much of it is asphalted, be aware that some segments, particularly on climbs like Collado Espina within the Collados de Asón Natural Park, can have mixed road conditions. You might encounter rough, bumpy sections, and short concrete stretches designed to aid grip on steep parts. There are also mentions of unpaved segments that may be difficult to navigate on a standard road bike, so be prepared for varied surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is celebrated for its diverse and picturesque scenery. Key highlights include the stunning views of the Santoña Marshes, which are ideal for bird watching. As you head inland, you'll experience the challenging ascent to Collado de Asón, offering rugged mountain views. Other notable spots include the Weir and slide at Coterillo on the Asón River and the Ampuero Climb.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route, you can start from various points. Good options for parking would be in the towns of Argoños or Ramales de la Victoria, which offer amenities and access to the route.

faqs.question: Are there any specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling this route, especially through natural parks?
faqs.answer: No specific cycling permits or entrance fees are generally required for this route, even when passing through natural parks like the Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel or the Collados de Asón Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations and protected areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring and autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures and generally favorable weather conditions for challenging road cycling in Cantabria, avoiding the intense heat of summer or the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through sections of several other paths, including the [CIMA CA08] Asón - La Sía, Camino del Val del Asón (Nacimiento del río Asón), and the Ruta de Carlos V. You'll also briefly encounter parts of the Senda del Litoral.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging racebike route, and bringing dogs along is generally not recommended due to the distance, elevation, and speed involved. While some areas might be accessible, it's not designed for cycling with pets.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns and villages where you can find amenities. Argoños, Ramales de la Victoria, and Santoña are good places to look for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options to refuel or stay overnight.

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding the unpaved segments mentioned for road bikes?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily for racebikes, some sections are noted as unpaved. These segments might be challenging or uncomfortable for standard road bike tires. It's advisable to check recent conditions or consider a bike with slightly wider tires if you're concerned, or be prepared to dismount and walk short sections if necessary.

waypoints.name: Weir and slide at Coterillo on the Asón River
